notes: US tight-oil proxy. Well cost = D&C+facilities per well across 5 US plays at 2014 well parameters, $4.9-8.3M (EIA/IHS Mar 2016 study; 2015 play averages $5.2-7.2M: Bakken 5.9, Eagle Ford 6.5, Marcellus 6.1, Midland 7.2, Delaware 5.2). Onshore exploration reference well US$3.71M total (2,000 m European well, Chatham House New Producers Group Exploration Cost Benchmark manual, 2019) confirms single-digit MUSD scale. Opex = lease operating expense $2.00-14.50/boe incl water disposal (EIA/IHS 2016). Schedule DERIVED: drilling <2 months + completion ~3-12 months, ND Bakken (EIA Today in Energy #41253, Sep 2019) => ~0.4-1.2 yr spud-to-first-production. capex/boe omitted: no accessible published onshore $/boe range found (context: Rystad Sep 2024 breakevens NAm shale $45/bbl, onshore Middle East $27/bbl).

Water depth <=400 m tied back to existing host (ERDA spec 10.4). Well cost: GoM shelf $12M for a 17,000-ft well, close to $50M for a 30,000-ft ultradeep-shelf well (Offshore Magazine, Jun 2004, 2004 USD - vintage caveat); shallow-water (50 m WD, 2,000 m TD) exploration reference well US$14.74M (Chatham House New Producers Group manual, 2019). capex/boe omitted - no shelf-specific public $/boe range found (context: Rystad Sep 2024 offshore-shelf breakeven $37/bbl; Rystad Dec 2023 shelf payback ~10 yr). Opex omitted - only GBP figures found: NSTA UKCS unit operating cost GBP 12/boe (2016) and GBP 19.49/boe (2024), not converted to USD. Schedule omitted - no defensible public range found; tiebacks are the fastest offshore class (see deepwater_tieback row).

capex/boe: Rystad via Offshore Magazine (Dec 2023) - offshore development cost/boe averaged $8 (2013-2022) vs 2013 high of $18; scope is offshore-wide (deepwater-focused article), applied to both deepwater rows. Wells: GoM deepwater D&C $60-240M for wells in 7,500-15,000 ft (EIA/IHS Mar 2016; Miocene $70-165M, Lower Tertiary $150-220M, Jurassic ~$230M); Rystad avg well cost in challenging/deepwater environments ~$50M (Offshore Magazine, Dec 2020). Schedule: Shell Kaikias ~2-yr sanction-to-first-oil (OE Digital, Apr 2017); WoodMac Jun 2019 - deepwater average FID-to-first-production now <3 yr; Chevron Ballymore FID 2022 -> first oil Apr 2025 (Offshore Magazine, May 2025). Breakeven context: WoodMac GoM tiebacks high-$20s-$30/bbl Brent vs standalone high-$40s-low-$50s (Apr 2017). Opex omitted - no accessible USD/bbl benchmark for tieback opex/host tariffs found.

capex/boe as deepwater row (Rystad via Offshore Magazine Dec 2023: offshore dev cost/boe $8 avg 2013-2022 vs $18 2013 high). Project anchors: Liza Ph1 $4.4B gross incl $1.2B FPSO lease capitalization for 120 kbd (Exxon PR 16 Jun 2017); Liza Ph2 $6B gross incl $1.6B FPSO lease capitalization for up to 220 kbd (Exxon PR 3 May 2019). Opex = Petrobras pre-salt lifting cost, 2024 quarterly range $6.28 (Q1) to $6.65 (Q4) per bbl (Zacks via Yahoo Finance) - best-in-class FPSO deepwater operator, NOT an industry-wide range. Schedule DERIVED from cited dates: Liza Ph1 FID 16 Jun 2017 -> first oil 20 Dec 2019 = ~2.5 yr; Liza Ph2 FID 3 May 2019 -> expected startup mid-2022 = ~3.2 yr (planned). Exxon: Liza first oil came 'less than five years after the first discovery... well ahead of the industry average for deepwater developments'. Wells: same GoM deepwater D&C $60-240M range (EIA/IHS Mar 2016).
